commitments.text: reduce emissions of carbon dioxide by 51% by 2030, and 70% conditionally
reduce emissions of carbon dioxide by 70% conditionally by 2030
reach net zero GHG emissions by 2030, conditionally
reach net zero GHG emissions by 2030, conditionally, with international finance and technology

measurability.reason: All commitments have specific numbers, units, target years, and baselines.

gap_findings.description: Mexico does not commit to a specific year for net zero GHG emissions, unlike the IPCC AR6 recommendation of 2050 for 1.5°C and 2070 for 2°C.
gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: IPCC AR6 WG3 SPM: global net zero CO2 by 2050 required for 1.5°C; net zero GHG by around 2070 for 2°C.

gap_findings.description: Mexico's NDC commitments are conditional on international finance and technology, which creates delivery risk.
gap_findings.ndc_evidence: reach net zero GHG emissions by 2030, conditionally, with international finance and technology
gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: IPCC AR6 WG3 SPM: global net zero CO2 by 2050 required for 1.5°C; net zero GHG by around 2070 for 2°C.

gap_findings.description: Mexico's NDC commitment to reduce emissions by 51% by 2030 is below the IPCC AR6 recommendation of 50% (26-64%) in 2050, relative to 2019.
gap_findings.ndc_evidence: reduce emissions of carbon dioxide by 51% by 2030
gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: IPCC AR6 WG3 Technical Summary: pathways following current NDCs until 2030 reach annual 50% (26-64%) in 2050, relative to 2019.

details.summary: Mexico's NDC commitments for net zero GHG emissions are incomplete and conditional, falling short of the IPCC AR6 recommendations for 1.5°C and 2°C pathways.

pass_c.country_summary: Mexico's NDC commitments for net zero GHG emissions are incomplete and conditional, falling short of the IPCC AR6 recommendations for 1.5°C and 2°C pathways.
pass_c.policy_recommendation: Mexico should commit to a specific year for net zero GHG emissions, such as 2050, and remove conditional dependencies on international finance and technology.
